provisions.title: Citation
provisions.content: These Regulations may be cited as the Land (Mortgage Financing) Regulations, 2009 and shall come into operations on the date of publication in the Gazette .

provisions.title: Application
provisions.content: These Regulations shall apply to mortgage of right of occupancy, lease or customary right of occupancy.

provisions.title: Interpretation
provisions.content: In these Regulations unless the context otherwise requires:— “ Act ” means the Land Act ; [Cap. 113] " applicant " means a person who makes a claim; " market price " means the "outstanding amount” means the balance of the principal sum of the loan not yet paid by the borrower; " possession application " means an application for the possession of mortgaged land, including building and parts of buildings; " reserve price " means the minimum price of a property to be sold in a public auction set by the mortgagee; and “ Tribunal ” means the District Land and Housing Tribunal. Part II – Mortgage of matrimonial home

provisions.title: Reasonable steps to ascertain matrimonial status of applicant for mortgage
provisions.content: (1) These applicant for a mortgage shall be required to declare his marital status as follows:— (a) by stating in the application form whether he is married or not; (b) subject to paragraph (a) where the applicant states that he is married, shall declare the names and address of the spouse or spouses as the case may be; and (c) where the applicant states that he is not married, the mortgagee shall require the applicant to declare in an affidavit or written and witnessed document that he has no spouse or any other third party holding interest in the mortgaged land. (2) A mortgagee may take such other steps in addition to the steps set out in these Regulations, as he considers necessary and desirable to satisfy himself as to the marital status of an applicant and such steps shall be made known to the applicant when he applies for a mortgage.

provisions.title: Assent by a spouse or spouses
provisions.content: (1) Where a matrimonial home is the subject of a mortgage, a mortgagee may satisfy himself that the assent by spouse or spouses is genuine and this duty shall be deemed to have been complied with if— (a) the mortgagee has in writing, advised the applicant for a mortgage that he should ensure that his spouse or spouses receive independent advice on the terms and conditions of the mortgage which is being applied for. and (b) the spouse or spouses shall provide a signed and witnessed document to the effect that they have received independent advice on the mortgage which is being applied for and have understood and assented to the terms and conditions of the mortgage or that they have, notwithstanding the advice from the firm, waived their opportunity to take independent advice. (2) A mortgagee may take such other steps in addition to the steps set out in this regulation, as he considers necessary, and desirable to satisfy himself that the assent of the spouse or spouses is informed and genuine. (3) The provisions of this regulation applies to a mortgage other than customary mortgage of a matrimonial home.

provisions.title: Assent by a spouse or spouses
provisions.content: (1) Where a matrimonial home is a subject of a customary mortgage, a mortgagee may satisfy’ himself that the assent of a spouse is an informed customary and genuine consent and this duty shall be deemed to have been complied mortgage with if: (a) the mortgagee has explained to the spouse or spouses of an applicant for a mortgage, in the presence of an independent person, the terms and conditions of the mortgage which is being applied for; and (b) the spouse or spouses, as the case may be, provide a signed and witnessed document or other clear evidence to the effect that they have received independent advice on the mortgage which is being applied for and have understood and assented to the terms and conditions of the mortgage. (2) In this regulation "independent person" means a person who is not related to, not involved in any joint business or commercial enterprise with and not a tenant or the landlord of the applicant for a mortgage. Part III – Conduct of mortgage business

provisions.title: Registration of mortgage
provisions.content: The Mortgagor shall be required during registration of the mortgage to submit the following documents to the Registrar:— (a) mortgage instruments; (b) certificate of title; (c) receipt of payment of stamp duty, registration fee, land rent or any other receipt which may be required; and (d) any other document that may be required by the Registrar.

provisions.title: Early repayment of a mortgage
provisions.content: Where the mortgagor wishes to make early repayment which may result into a discharge of the mortgage, the mortgagee may charge early repayment fee, provided that such fee shall not exceed one month interest of the outstanding amount.

provisions.title: Transfer of mortgage
provisions.content: (1) Where the mortgagor wishes to transfer a mortgage, he shall seek consent of the mortgagee. (2) Subject to subregulation (1) , the mortgagee shall within reasonable time give his consent or refusal for transfer of mortgage.

provisions.title: Fixing of reserve price
provisions.content: Where a mortgage land is sold in a public auction, the mortgagee or receiver may fix a reserve price based on the market price .

provisions.title: Sale by mortgage to himself
provisions.content: Where the mortgagee has sold to himself the mortgaged land, he shall be required to provide the following evidence to the Registrar:— (a) leave of the court, if sale was concluded by a private treaty; (b) where the sale was concluded through a public auction, a certificate of sale;

provisions.title: Redemp­tion of a mortgage
provisions.content: The mortgagor may redeem his mortgaged land any time before acceptance of a bid in a public auction or on execution of sale agreement under a private treaty. Part IV – Disputes

provisions.title: Application of this Part
provisions.content: This Part shall apply to any dispute by a mortgagee or mortgagor or any person having interest in any mortgaged land, in respect of the following matters: (a) payment of monies secured by the mortgage; (b) sale of the mortgaged land; (c) delivery of possession of the mortgaged land to the mortgagee by the mortgagor or any other person who is or is alleged to'be in possession of the mortgaged land; (d) the discharge of the land from the security; or (e) delivery of possession of the land by the mortgagee to the mortgagor; (f) an action by a mortgagor for an account by a receiver of how the receiver has applied any moneys received under section 128 of the Act ; (g) an action by the mortgagor against a mortgagee for an account by the mortgagee in respect of the conduct of his powers of: (i) a leasing the mortgaged land under section 129 of the Act ; (ii) a selling the mortgaged land under section 132 through section 134 of the Act ; (h) any application brought by one mortgagee against another mortgagee in a situation where land is subject to more than one mortgage and there is a dispute between the mortgages arising out of or connected to their respective mortgages; and (i) any other application arising out of or connected to a mortgage or mortgaged land.

provisions.title: Particulars of the claim for payment of moneys or possession of land
provisions.content: Where the applicant claims as mortgagee for payment of monies secured by a mortgage of land or possession of such land, the particulars of claim shall include in addition to the matters specified in regulation 15 . the following: (a) the date of the mortgage; (b) the identification of the land sought to be recovered; (c) where possession of the land is claimed, whether or not the land includes:— (i) a dwelling house in which any person is in residence; (ii) any land in actual, use for agriculture purposes or (iii) any land in actual use for pastoral purposes.

provisions.title: Mortgage claim land in use and occupation
provisions.content: (1) This regulation applies where a claimant as mortgagee possession of land to which regulation 14 applies. (2) Where the claimant’s claim is brought because of a failure to make the periodic payments due under the mortgage, the particulars of claim shall:— (a) give details of all payments which have been missed altogether; (b) give details of any other payments required to be made as a term of the mortgage, such as insurance premiums, legal costs, default interest, penalties, administrative or other charges, together with any other sums claimed stating the nature and amount of each such charge, whether any payment is in arrear and whether or not it is included in the amount of any periodic payment; and (c) give such relevant information as is known by the claimant about the defendant’s circumstances. Part IV – Miscellaneous

provisions.title: Ammendment of GN. No. 74 of 2001
provisions.content: The Land (Disposition of Right of Occupancy) Regulations are hereby amended by deleting regulation 3 (b) and the words "by a prescribed lender" appearing in regulation 4(2) (b).

provisions.title: Revocation of GN. No. 43 of 2006
provisions.content: The Land (Mortgage) Regulations are hereby revoked. Schedule Forms [Please note: Forms have not been reproduced. definitions.definition: a person who makes a claim
the "outstanding amount” means the balance of the principal sum of the loan not yet paid by the borrower
an application for the possession of mortgaged land, including building and parts of buildings
the minimum price of a property to be sold in a public auction set by the mortgagee
the District Land and Housing Tribunal. Part II – Mortgage of matrimonial home
